summary: The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on case BZA 2024-004, an area variance request for property at 2588 McHenry Road. The applicant, Mr. Helton, sought to reduce the required side yard setback from 10 feet to 5 feet in order to build a new home (two bedrooms and a two-car garage) on his R1-zoned property. Discussion centered on whether the new structure would be attached or detached from an existing workshop/garage structure, with the board advising the applicant that a detached structure would require a new single-family home permit. The board discussed conditions including limiting the structure to no more than 60 feet in length to maintain setback compliance, and the variance was considered for approval.
decisions: Approval of previous meeting minutes from July 15, 2024 (passed with one abstention from Mr. Segal)
Area variance BZA 2024-004 to reduce side yard setback from 10 feet to 5 feet for property at 2588 McHenry Road appears to have been granted with condition that structure not exceed 60 feet in length
action_items: Max Merritt (Zoning Consultant) to review final submitted plans to ensure compliance with 5-foot side yard and 10-foot opposite side yard setbacks and 60-foot length maximum
Township zoning staff to process applicant's permit - either as building addition ($200) or new home ($750) depending on whether structure is attached or detached
Board members to recruit two alternates for BZA and Zoning Commission
key_amounts: $550 difference between addition and new home permit
1,400 square feet minimum floor area required for single-family dwelling
723 square foot two-car garage proposed in updated plan
5-foot proposed side yard setback (reduced from 10-foot requirement)
60-foot maximum structure length discussed as condition
notable_topics: Area variance request BZA 2024-004 for reduced side yard setback at 2588 McHenry Road
Discussion of whether new structure should be attached or detached from existing building
Discrepancies between submitted site plan and floor plan dimensions
Existing structure not meeting minimum 1,400 sq ft residential standard
Accessory structure rules for properties over two acres in residential zoning
Recruitment of alternates for BZA and Zoning Commission boards
attendees: Zach Helton (Adjacent property owner / applicant's son)
notes: This is a Board of Zoning Appeals (BZA) meeting, not a Board of Trustees meeting, despite repeated transcript artifacts referencing 'Board of Trustees Meeting.' The transcript contains numerous garbled/corrupted text segments likely from poor audio transcription or automated captioning errors. Mr. Pruitt was noted as absent. The applicant's existing structure is described as essentially a garage/workshop rather than a true residence, and the applicant has contested property taxes annually because of this. Next meeting scheduled for September 16, 2024. Meeting adjourned at 7:01 PM.
title: BZA 8-19-24
